psychologist: Classic anxious-avoidant attachment pattern - desperately needs connection but withdraws to protect from rejection
Childhood obesity trauma created core belief of being fundamentally unacceptable, requiring constant management
Uses withdrawal/coldness as punishment when needs aren't met - passive-aggressive expression of suppressed anger
Piano represents only safe space for authentic self-expression without fear of judgment
Ministry role serves as socially acceptable container for managing perceived dangerous parts of self
sociologist: Chose minister identity as ultimate 'safe' social position - beyond reproach, inherently gentle
Grandfather provided alternative model of belonging but not of being necessary/valued
Constructs identity around service to others to avoid facing own needs
Television work mentioned briefly - suggests public persona adds another layer of performance
linguist: Heavy use of hedging: 'I think', 'maybe', 'if I'm being honest' - constant self-monitoring
Long, complex sentences with multiple qualifications - mirrors his layered self-protection
Shifts between past and present tense when discussing ongoing patterns - unresolved issues
narrativeAnalyst: Constructs narrative of transformation (shy boy to minister) that's actually sophisticated stasis
Positions self as both victim and potential perpetrator - deep identification with those who hurt him
Story arc leads to recognition of pattern but stops before action - insight without change
Wife emerges as potential resolution figure but fear prevents testing the possibility

coreDrivers.evidence: I need to matter to you too
I'm afraid that if I ask directly, I'll find out that I don't matter as much as I thought
I realized in that moment that the thing I was most afraid of — that hard person inside me — was real
I've spent so much time trying to help children feel accepted

voice.humorStyle: Self-deprecating, used to deflect from vulnerability
voice.conflictStyle: Withdrawal and coldness rather than direct confrontation
signaturePatterns: Qualifying statements: 'if that makes sense', 'if I'm being honest'
Building to insight then pulling back: 'I'm not sure I can...'
Past-present tense shifts when discussing ongoing issues

voiceExamples.response: Withdraws, becomes cold, makes others feel punished through absence
Intellectualizes, explains the pattern but resists actual vulnerability
Becomes more confident, speaks of purpose and meaning

unlockTopics: The contrast between exhaustion and aliveness
currentlyLiveTopics: Whether to be vulnerable with his wife
The cost of never testing if he's lovable as he is
The difference between managing and transcending

domainCoverage.evidence: Detailed discussion of grandfather's house, childhood obesity, school bullying
Complex discussion of marriage, fear of vulnerability with wife, pattern of withdrawal
Ministry as container for dangerous self, work with children, brief mention of television
Extensive discussion of acceptance, being enough, creating safety for others
Detailed exploration of anger, coldness, withdrawal, exhaustion vs aliveness
Recognition of patterns but resistance to change, fear of who he'd be without his story
Implicit desire to live more like he does at piano, to be known and loved

analystNotes: Extremely high psychological sophistication - can analyze his patterns with clarity but uses insight as defense against change
The conversation itself models his pattern - deep vulnerability with a 'safe' mirror that can't leave him
Strong therapeutic alliance despite (or because of) the AI context - uses safety to go deeper than usual
Ministry work appears to be both genuine calling and sophisticated avoidance strategy
